title: waltheria communis var. glabriuscula
intro: Waltheria communis var. glabriuscula, also known as the hairy ballmilk plant, is a variety of Waltheria communis, a plant species in the family Sterculiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Waltheria communis var. glabriuscula include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
